body_txt: A cousin to Taking You with Me and a technique used by the Cornered Rattlesnake , a Suicide Attack is an attack where the attacker has every intention of sacrificing themself to destroy their target. Suicide attacks are mostly associated with explosives nowadays, either strapped to the body or packed in a vehicle. However, this trope is actually Older Than Steam , with the first recorded example occurring in 162 BC. Truth in Television , needless to say (terrorists do this all the freakin' time ). Some involuntary instances are cases of Why Am I Ticking? . Action Bomb is when this is treated as a Heroic Sacrifice rather than a case of We Have Reserves . Unlike Why Am I Ticking? (where the person rigged with explosives has been so rigged against their will, and is perhaps unaware of it until the kaboom) it's voluntary either way. When the trope is considered to be Suicide Attack, though, the bomber has usually been indoctrinated to believe it to be noble and just. Needless to say, this means that the Suicide Attacker is more often than not a terrorist suicide bomber, or a mook for an Evil Empire that has reserves , and either way they're a Card-Carrying Villain . (The Redshirt Army sometimes uses Suicide Attacks too, though if the good guys are resorting to this, the work is probably using Grey-and-Gray Morality at best.) An Action Bomb is the straight-up heroic version, who blows themself up to make absolutely certain that their explosive is killing the Big Bad or one of their most dangerous subordinates , or outright destroying the Big Bad 's lair/superweapon/plans/army/evil stuff. Furthermore, the Action Bomb resorts to this after considering all other options and deciding that they just won't do enough damage to the enemy in a timely enough fashion, or more commonly, when circumstances such as being under attack or captured by the bad guys prevent the formulation or execution of a less risky plan. Finally, the Action Bomb sometimes survives their attack, while the Suicide Attack is in virtually all cases doesn't. In realistic settings the Suicide Attack is almost always an explosion but in fantastic settings it can take many other forms. Compare Cast from Hit Points in which life must be sacrificed and perhaps irreparably damaged, but not necessarily ended. This is closely related to Murder-Suicide and Taking You with Me . Can be combined with External Combustion if the attacker drives a vehicle filled with explosives to their target. Contrast with Suicide Mission , where survival is unlikely but self-sacrifice is not mandatory.
